summary: Global markets are navigating a risk-on shift as hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire deal dominate sentiment, with equities gaining and oil prices falling sharply — crude dropping over 2% — on expectations that a framework agreement could be finalized as early as Friday following Trump's confirmation of an imminent decision. The Iran conflict has been the dominant macro force of May, triggering wild swings in global bonds, disrupting oil supply chains from Japan to the Philippines, and prompting ECB research warning of a potential double scarring effect on eurozone consumers. In equities, SpaceX's anticipated IPO is generating significant speculative momentum, with traders pricing in near-certainty that Elon Musk becomes the world's first trillionaire, while Replimune is in focus after announcing plans to resubmit its twice-rejected drug following recent FDA restructuring. Key risks investors are monitoring include the possibility that any US-Iran deal proves as ineffective as the 2015 JCPOA, ongoing Strait of Hormuz access uncertainty, geopolitical complications involving Pakistan and broader regional diplomacy, and crypto-adjacent volatility as Kalshi expands into digital asset trading lanes. In the short term, markets will be highly sensitive to Friday's Iran deal outcome, with a confirmed ceasefire likely sustaining the oil selloff and equity rally, while any breakdown in talks could rapidly reverse these gains and reignite safe-haven flows into gold and bonds.
